name: setTimerCyclic
title: setTimerCyclic
syntax: void setTimerCyclic(msTimer t, long firstDuration, long period); // form 1
void setTimerCyclic(msTimer t, long period); // form 2
void setTimerCyclic(timer t, int64 periodInNs); // form 3
description: With form 2, firstDuration is implicitly the same as period, i.e. the timer runs precisely according to period the first time.

parameters.name: firstDuration
parameters.description: Time in milliseconds until the timer runs out for the first time.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: Time in milliseconds in which the timer is restarted in case of expiration.

parameters.name: periodInNs
parameters.description: Time in nanoseconds in which the timer is restarted in case of expiration.

description: Starting of a timer that expires the first time 10 ms after the start of measurement and thereafter every 20 ms (10 ms, 30 ms, 50 ms, 70 ms etc.)

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units
